SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper presents a technically coherent approach: it formulates the expected tour length objective (Eq. 3), derives policy-gradient updates via REINFORCE (Eq. 4–5), proposes an actor-critic baseline (Alg. 1) and an Active Search procedure (Alg. 2), and supplies extensive empirical comparisons against Christofides, OR-Tools and Concorde. The experimental methodology uses sensible baselines, multiple problem sizes (TSP20/50/100) and ablations (sampling, greedy, active search), supporting the main claims. Some minor concerns remain: results report averages but no statistical significance tests and runtime comparisons mix GPU and CPU implementations, which can bias conclusions about speed; the supervised baseline underperformance is noted but attributed to implementation subtleties rather than analyzed rigorously. Overall the evidence supports the claims but a few methodological details (statistical tests, fairer time comparisons) are missing.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The work creatively combines Pointer Networks (Vinyals et al.) with model-free policy gradients and an actor-critic baseline to train for combinatorial optimization, and introduces the Active Search idea of refining policy parameters at test time — a non-trivial extension beyond prior supervised pointer-network work. The problem (TSP, knapsack) is well-studied, but applying RL in this manner and demonstrating competitive empirical results is a novel perspective relative to the cited supervised approaches. The contribution is not a fundamentally new learning paradigm, but is a notable and meaningful synthesis and extension of existing techniques that would inform experts.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (intro, related work, architecture, optimization, experiments, appendix) and clearly states the main ideas early (RL pretraining vs Active Search). Notation is mostly consistent and algorithms are presented concretely (Alg. 1–2); architectural and training details are provided in the appendix. There are minor presentation issues (typos like “availabe”, “architecural”) and some referenced figures/tables are not shown in the provided excerpt, but overall the exposition is clear and reproducible from the described methods and hyperparameters.
